reviews: What a find! This stall is highly recommended by some of the elderly residents living at Margaret drive who were seen queuing, two hours before the stall even opened for business at 5.30pm. The prized dish here is undoubtedly their addictive deep-fried pork chop with the crispiest biscuit-like finish I have ever eaten! 🤩 #burpple #burpplesg #stfoodtrending #straitstimesfood #sgfood #sgeats #foodsg #wheretoeatsg #whattoeatsg #sghawker #hawkersg #margaretdrivehawkercentre
stalls.stall_name: Jing Shu Shi Curry Rice 京熟食咖喱饭 (Queenstown)

contact_info.address: 38A Margaret Drive, #02-31 Margaret Drive Hawker Centre, Singapore 142038

google.google_name: 京熟食咖喱饭 Jing Shu Shi Curry Rice

reviews.author: AC Chan
reviews.text:
What a find! This stall is highly recommended by some of the elderly residents living at Margaret drive who were seen queuing, two hours before the stall even opened for business at 5.30pm.
The prized dish here is undoubtedly their addictive deep-fried pork chop with the crispiest biscuit-like finish I have ever eaten! 🤩

reviews.author: owen
reviews.text:
Decided to give this a go. I havn't had many good run-ins with curry rice, and unfortunately;  this continued the streak.

Ordered a minimalistic rice with sweet and sour pork and bittergourd, topped with curry sauce.

Curry - a huge letdown. It had an overly watery consistency and taste also strangely did not taste like curry. An overly bland orange sauce more like.

Bittergourd was a disappointment as well. It was the super thin kind which had no proper taste when bitten on, felt very limp. The egg paired with it did little to elevate the taste.

Lastly for the sweet and sour pork, very basic kind but leans more to the ones vegetarians use - a more mild version.

Overll at $3.50, this is nowhere cheap. Neither is it that good. 2 BODs that I can give could be my choice of dishes chosen, or it just suits old people tastebuds - Many old people were queueing which did add to the hype.

Can't say I found anything redeemable or worth coming back to for this place.

reviews.author: Boon Yeo
reviews.text:
The dishes are excellent, but the curry is absolutely horrible. Think of this place as a caifan stall and DON'T GET THE CURRY and it'll be all good. Don't say I didn't warn you! It's watery and tasteless, worse than Roti Prata Curry. The pork chop and vegetables I got were 5/5 so overall 4/5.

Alternatively you could order curry from another stall and pour it on and ta-dah you have real curry rice!

reviews.author: Adrian Chan
reviews.text: Simple plate of food. My plate cost 4.30 which can be considered slightly higher for economical rice, but the food is quite good. Hawker was quite empty at dinner time so its quite easy to get a table

reviews.author: John
reviews.text: This is the best curry rice or cai fan in Queenstown

reviews: This plate of roast chicken drumstick with glistening and appetising golden-brown skin is surprisingly tender and moist — and goes perfectly well with the tasty soya sauce dressing over the chicken! 🫶🏻 #burpple #burpplesg #stfoodtrending #straitstimesfood #sgfood #sgeats #foodsg #wheretoeatsg #whattoeatsg #sghawker #hawkersg #margaretdrivehawkercentre #tongkeechickenrice

You should find many familiar stalls in Margaret Drive Food Centre as they are mostly moved here from the former Tanglin Halt Food Centre. Tong Kee Chicken Rice is one of them and you could immediately see a long queue in front of this stall when you are here. Previously they only sell roasted chicken rice and now at this new place, they have since selling white version as well. I do love their roasted chicken rice, fragrant enough and the meat is soft.

This stall only has roast chicken. Luckily, although I'm a firm believer that chicken rice should be white, I came for their roast chicken. Unfortunately, the bird was quite disappointing. It's not unlike most roast chicken. Shout out to the rice though. Very fragrant.

It’s not your usual chicken rice, but the lor chap served with it really gives it a special twist. Has a 'wok hay' fragrance. Try this when you're sick of your normal chicken rice, roasted chicken only. Add egg $0.50

I felt that for chicken rice ($3.00) (breast) was quite dry and not much taste. I saw most of people changed to chicken drumstick or wings instead + add-on char siew ($1.00) was marinated well with the sauce and having strong roasted taste.
Tong kee was previously stationed at commonwealth hawker centre along Margaret drive, which was just beside my secondary school. The roasted chicken was juicy, left the juicy skin last. Yums 👍👍
stalls.stall_name: Tong Kee Chicken Rice (Margaret Drive Hawker Centre)

contact_info.address: 38A Margaret Drive, #01-36 Margaret Drive Hawker Centre, Singapore 142038

google.google_name: Tong Kee Chicken Rice 东记鸡饭

reviews.author: rn_1604
reviews.text:
Rice is fragrant but the chicken sauce overpowered it until it taste like normal rice. The sauce is too salty. Chicken be roasted or steamed is ok.
Queuing at 12:30pm on weekends takes about 35 mins.

reviews.author: Winston Ho
reviews.text:
This stall has been around for a long time. Previously at Commonwealth Food Center, they are now located at tgw ground floor of the ‘new’ Margaret Drive Food Center.

They lie signature item is their fried whole chicken, which chicken rice stalls term as roast chicken. They also have roast pork and char siew available.

My go-to order is their chicken breast. You must ask for their special sauce which is house-made and it tastes like teriyaki sauce. It goes really well with the chicken and rice.

Their ginger sauce is also good, alongside their chili sauce.

There are usually long queues during lunch time.

Highly recommended!

😋👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻

reviews.author: Isaac Sam
reviews.text:
This chicken rice stall is no hidden secret—the perpetually long queue, especially during lunch hours, speaks volumes of its popularity among regulars and curious newcomers alike. Offering both roasted and steamed chicken, each portion is consistently tender, succulent, and well-executed, with just the right balance of seasoning and moisture.

While the rice could do with a touch more fragrance and richness, it serves its purpose as a neutral base, letting the other elements shine. The real standout, however, is the chilli sauce—undeniably the star of the show. Zesty, punchy, and with just the right amount of heat, it elevates the entire plate and leaves a lasting impression.

Efficient service despite the crowds ensures that turnover is brisk, and the stall’s consistency makes it worth the wait. A dependable choice for a satisfying, classic local meal around Margaret Drive/Dawson area.

reviews.author: Adrian
reviews.text: I see a long queue for this chicken rice stall, I ordered 2 wings + egg at the price of $5.80. Overall, it's not bad, but I have been eating chicken rice for so many years. I feel about 7/10. Not super fantastic . Can try if you are there.

reviews.author: Tee Yongnan
reviews.text:
Tong Kee Chicken Rice (东记鸡饭) serves one of the best chicken rice I have ever eaten. The long queue, a testament to the quality of their food since 1969, is well worth the wait.

I ordered the $6 portion of roasted chicken rice with add on roast pork and braised egg, total of $7.80.

The portion size is super generous, featuring huge chunks of boneless chicken meat that are perfectly roasted. Each piece boasts a crisp skin while remaining tender and juicy inside. The rice is incredibly fragrant, with a delightful aroma that isn't overly oily, making each bite light yet flavorful, perfectly complementing the richness of the chicken.

Despite the lengthy line, the queue moves relatively fast, thanks to the efficient staff. Tong Kee Chicken Rice has truly mastered the art of delivering quality food swiftly, making it a must-visit spot for any chicken rice aficionado.

reviews: Old School Wanton Mee@$4. +$2 for 2 additional dumplings. Noodles were springy and el dente. Dumplings were meaty & flavourful. Their char siew is the dry and lean type, so don't expect anything special. I would say this is a slightly above average WTM. Tian Xiang Wanton Noodle used to be located at the now demolished Tanglin Halt food centre.

Wanton Noodles ($3.50). Prices have gone up slightly but it's still very cheap! I've reviewed this before - it's not say *super* good but it's something we keep going back to, it's good enough. I prefer the black sauce one (this is the white one), bc the noodles are a bit alkaliny and the black sauce masks that. We opt for no charsiew bc it's quite dry. Fried wantons are crispy and boiled wantons are slippery smooth, not very alkaliny. Noodles are springy and very nice when piping hot! Green chilli is a must.

Old School Wanton Noodles. When you get quite sick of cooking and washing up , what better than a takeout of a classic good old wanton mee. Loving the wanton mee here . Every component was done well . Lean and tasty char siew , springy noodles , a dip of crisp wanton and juicy steamed wantons and the best part - that wicked homemade chilli which is so addictive .

Wanton Mee. This was pretty good, but I feel like what stands out the most to me in this store is the crispy fried wantons. Gives a very satisfying crunch! They are also very generous with the wantons - 5 boiled in soup, 5 fried wantons.

My $3.50 plate contained a considerable mound of noodle accompanied by char siew, both fried and boiled wantons as well as some chye sim. . The noodle is flavorful while its texture is delightfully springy without the alkaline smell. I love the old school taste which reminded me of the wanton noodle that I had as a child. . What's admirable is that this plate of wanton noodle is actually prepared by a young chap probably in his late 20s. . Read more at: https://thedeadcockroach.blogspot.com/2019/02/tian-xiang-wanton-noodle-tanglin-halt.html?m=1
stalls.stall_name: Tian Xiang Wanton Noodle 天香云吞面 (Queenstown)

contact_info.address: 38A Margaret Drive, #02-41 Margaret Drive Hawker Centre, Singapore 141001

reviews.author: Stanley Chin Guo Qiang
reviews.text:
Good priced wanton mee with decent portions.

Wanton mee has very little starchy aftertaste with the saucing on a light side, different from the usual wanton mee.

Boiled and fried wantons have good texture and fillings

Would say overall good one around Queenstown area

reviews.author: G L
reviews.text: Really enjoyed the wonton noodles here! Noodles are perfectly cooked and the seasoning is light (not the dark heavy sauce kind) but very tasty and flavourful. Tasted just like the good old days!

reviews.author: Ken Chua
reviews.text: Tried the dry wanton noodles here. Not too bad except that the fried wantons were rather small and dry. The char siew are tasty while the noodles were quite q and fragrant.

reviews.author: ptylink link
reviews.text:
1st time at this hawker centre.
Ordered the dry wanton mee without chilli $4, it is nice, noodle is not soggy, 3 fried wanton freshly done. Will recommend for those looking for traditional taste! Q is fast, was here at 12.20pm on Saturday.. Stall is on the 2nd level, at the corner.

reviews.author: Zaggie Ng
reviews.text:
Sunday visit @ 10.15AM, 29/9/24

Affirms the starchy noodles. They used to serve good stringy noodles. Perhaps the staff changed and failed to wash off the starch properly after boiling.

Good chilli. Soup was very bland. Serves the lean kind of char siew. Appreciate the option to pay with QR code. Affordable $4 bowl.

reviews: One of the popular food stall at Margaret Drive Hawker Centre that the queue always long, serving their authentic Hakka style thunder tea rice and yong tau fu. Since their yong tau fu also one of the popular item here, so I grab this instead. You get to choose the fried items or veggie pieces you want. I would say the fried items are so crispy and delicious. Get about 4 pieces should be enough to complete it as a meal.

Their Thunder Tea Rice comes with a lot of ingredients in a bowl, and you also get to add Yong Tau Foo (each pc $1) to the bowl. I added on 1 Meat Ball and 1 mushroom, which was really not bad! Let's start with the Thunder Tea Rice - with so much ingredients in the bowl, and especially with the crunch from ikan bilis and peanuts, there'll be a lot going on, and you'll start to realise the bowl seems to be never-ending. Certainly a huge portion, and I enjoyed pairing with the chilli/ sweet sauce (self-service). Really recommend getting their YTF, which they were constantly frying, so you know it's freshly made. The meatball was crispy on the outside, and the meat was really juicy and flavourful!

The queue for this is crazy. It moves very slowly, I spent about 1 hour queueing for this because the sunk cost fallacy kicked in. Started at ~11.45am on a Sunday. It's quite good! There's ikan bilis, mani cai, long beans, leeks, cabbage, peanuts, firm tofu cubes and cai po. I added on a fried taukwa (which was huge). Each component was done quite well, textures and flavours were great - peanuts/ikan/longbeans were crunchy, veg/tofu were soft with a bite, and the taukwa was freshly. The soup was quite palatable, not too bitter but not as nutty as lindama's.

I was searching for it in Tanglin Halt Market but to no avail. Actually it's at Tanglin Halt Food Centre which is 3min walk away. I ordered thunder tea brown rice ($4 - has chopped veggies, tofu, ikan bilis, peanut, soup is lightly bitter but wholesome and hearty) and the handmade yong tau fu which are filled generously with minced meat/fish paste (1 each of all YTF, $6.40). The soup is very flavourful and the chilli is sedap!

I ordered a bowl of thunder tea with white rice for $3.50. . There are a medley of ingredients in my bowl including chye poh (preserved radish), tau kwa, fried ikan bilis, peanuts and some kind of vegetable that I was not able to identify. . The bowl is rather light on flavors as the chye poh is the sweet variety. However, the seasoning from the vegetable makes up for it. . While everything was chopped up for easy eating, the ikan bilis and peanuts added some crunch and texture to the dish.
stalls.stall_name: Hakka Thunder Tea Rice (Margaret Drive Hawker Centre)

contact_info.address: 38A Margaret Drive, #02-34 Margaret Drive Hawker Centre, Singapore 142038

reviews.author: Tan Sindy
reviews.text:
Very clean and spacious hawker.
$8 for brown rice thunder tea + 2pcs yong toufu.
The thunder tea come with a lot of peanuts and vege. I like to pour in the green soup and enjoy the wholesome flavourful herbal taste of thunder tea.
Yong toufu is a good large pcs with fish paste and deep fried.
Ordered the tau pok type to absorb soup for savour at once.

reviews.author: Mark Yeo
reviews.text: Followed friend's recommendation and tried their thunder tea set and also 4 pieces of hakka yong tau foo. Absolutely flavourful and yummy rice bowl. The YTF had substantial filling and was well fried. Its a good meal. The queue scenario confirms its popularity and how yummy the food is.

reviews.author: Monda S.
reviews.text:
My favourite Thunder Tea 擂茶 on the whole island 😃! I do have to say, I prefer it when the husband, who owns the store, makes it. I haven’t tried the other location where he’s there yet. This is my go-to for an authentic Thunder Tea experience. I loved their freshly prepared soup base, and the fried sides are great too.

You just have to wait.

reviews.author: Evin Yeap
reviews.text: Try Hakka Thunder Tea @Margaret Drive Hawker Center for lunch, the Yong Toufu is the best with different combinations, queue more than 30mins but worthwhile, lady boss is very amicable

reviews.author: Ken Chua
reviews.text: My wife told me the queue moves really slowly. There were also many takeaway orders. And some lady just cut queue and went to the front to order 6 or 7 bowls which prolonged the waiting time. The stall owner seems to know her and accepted her orders without consideration for the people waiting patiently in the long line of queue. This is really unfair! Told my wife to take her business elsewhere next time.

reviews: Formerly opened at Tanglin Halt FC. DJ satay grill have since shifted to Margaret Drive Hawker Centre. Chicken Satay was soft and tender and the meats were Chunkier in size then some other satays stalls I had before.

contact_info.address: 38a Margaret Drive, #02-22 Margaret Drive Hawker Centre, Singapore 142038

reviews.author: Inka M
reviews.text:
Sadly, no satay available anymore, but ordered Nasi lemak instead. Everything was tasty and the sambal one of the best I've had.

Small, but very affordable portion. Smiling and friendly customer service!

reviews.author: Wong
reviews.text: Waited about 10 minutes for the satay, but it was well worth it! The best my husband and I have ever eaten. Minimum order is 10 sticks, and we tried a mix of beef, mutton, and chicken. All the meat was tender, juicy, and well-marinated, with no burnt parts. Truly delicious!

reviews.author: Alvin Quah
reviews.text:
Ordered 20 chicken Satay to satisfy our carving. They were really delicious, nicely marinated and pretty well BBQed.

A little more burnt will be nice but it was good as it is. It is served with diced cucumber and onions.

If you need a good satay fix, this is a great place to get it.

reviews.author: Adrian Heng
reviews.text: Satay lovers rejoice. This is stall is top class for satay. The chicken was tender and juicy and the mutton equally mouthwatering and grilled to perfection.  I paired the satay with the chicken cutlet rice which was really nicely fried with long grain rice.

reviews.author: Travis Lee
reviews.text:
Satays are cooked on the spot. Sizzling and piping hot. Crispy on the edges and juicy on the inside.

Service from the store owners were very friendly and nice.

I would come again for late night satay snacks!

reviews: Their beloved rendition features a warm thin clear soup / broth, infused with chicken, turmeric, lemongrass, galangal, and other herbs and spices, stewed over hours. . The layered and robust meaty savoury sweet earthy herbal salty spice flavour of the soup / broth is wholesome and nuanced. . The base of chewy yellow egg noodles, crisp bean sprouts, tender boneless shredded chicken, and crisp coriander, carry grainy sweet vegetal herbal meaty savoury flavour. . The 2 unique additions are the crisp fried anchovies with fishy salty savoury flavour, and the thin vinegared chili with bright sour spicy flavour.

Regularly sold out well before their official close of business each day, this beloved stall has been serving their hearty rendition since the 1960s. . The generous portion has a base of dense chewy soft compressed rice cakes with grainy sweet flavour, in a rich creamy broth of turmeric, galangal, lemongrass, coconut milk, toasted grated coconut, and other spices, resulting in a punchy sweet savoury earthy spice flavour.

Queenstown Lontong is a popular store at Magaret Drive Hawker Centre. Queues get rather long during weekend breakfasts. Other than their Lontong, their Mee Rebus also popular here. Everybody in queue; would order Mee Rebus or Lontong. Mee Rebus was rich in flavours. Gravy was thick and flavourful. Ikan bilis was excellent. Bergedil was quite good as well
$3 mee rebus, the gravy is a bit on the sweet side but full of yummy peanut flavour
Mee hoon Soto $3, superb Soto full of flavour and goes great with bergedil
Mee Rebus - The gravy was rich, savoury and had a lovely hint of sweetness. Read more: https://www.misstamchiak.com/queenstown-lontong/

contact_info.address: 38A Margaret Drive, #01-27 Margaret Drive Hawker Centre, Singapore 142038

reviews.author: Ken Chua
reviews.text: Came to try their lontong and nasi lemak but both were already sold out by noon time. Not surprising given the long queue. We ordered the mee suam and mee rebus instead. Quite tasty but a bit salty.

reviews.author: Isman Tanuri
reviews.text:
Posting this on 8/1/2026 as feedback for owners. We've been coming here over the last couple of years from east side. Because the lontong was really very good, thick, rich and flavourful. Mee rebus was also very enjoyable.

However, today was a massive disappointment. Lontong was tasteless and gravy, watery. Mee rebus was just oily (I can't remember ever having oily mee rebus elsewhere.) Very different compared to our experiences over the years.

We hope today is a one-off letdown although it'll be a while before we come here again. If this is the new standard, I'm afraid another lost culinary legacy is on the cards.

reviews.author: Victor Chan
reviews.text: Had the lontong and added begedil and chicken wing. The lontong gravy had a  rich traditional flavor with generous serving of ikan bills which provided a crunchy texture, accompaniedvery well with the sweet chilli sambal. Enjoyed the hearty meal,  possibly one of the best lontong I had. Will be back to sample the mee rebus, mee soto, nasi lemak, ... etc.

reviews.author: K Tan
reviews.text:
What a hidden gem for lontong at Margaret Drive Food Centre!
Totally wasn’t expecting it to be this good. The lontong and mee rebus are on point — gravy is thick, rich, super fragrant, and loaded with flavour.
Generous portion for just $4, plus heaps of crispy fried anchovies on top 🤤
Service is friendly and super fast too.
Legit shiok. Worth the trip. 💯

reviews.author: Toh Lawson
reviews.text:
Had always wanted to try the longing for the longest time and finally had one today!

The queue is relatively short as it’s a weekday. No regret ordered the longtong and one fried chicken wing to try, the longtong is generously topped with ikan billies, fried dry coconut shreds and the sweet spicy sambal! I’ve asked for less rice cakes and added $1 vegetables!

The fried ikan billies are fresh, crispy and tasty with the lemak gravy is an excellent combination of heaven and earth where crispy crips meet the gravy which no other stalls offer!

Rice cakes are soft and yummy texture though it is the “plastic” packed type! The lontong vegetables are cooked flavoured coconut gravy and the lomba spices added to the whole gravy is awesome and delicious!

One of the BEST longtong you should try if you’re nearby Queenstown area! 9/10 scores!!!

Fried chicken wing is flavourful and crispy! 8/10 scores!

reviews: Whilst Margaret Drive Hawker Centre is one of the newer hawker centres to have opened in the West of Singapore, the hawker centre does seem to house some really interesting and unique concepts within the complex. Shrimp Wonton Noodle (Dry) comes with bamboo noodles, piquant spiciness of the chili, the slurpy skin and sufficient filling of the prawn wontons, and the lightly savoury and mildly peppery soup.
stalls.stall_name: Hey Canton Wonton Noodle (Margaret Drive Hawker Centre)

contact_info.address: 38A Margaret Drive, #02-33 Margaret Drive Hawker Centre, Singapore 142038

google.google_name: 廣府仔雲吞麵 HEY CANTON WONTON NOODLE

reviews.author: T greensake
reviews.text:
25/Feb/2026
Had wanton noodle dry ($5.80), was normal. Soup & noodle was bland for me, chilli/condiments used not to my liking. Wanton was quite good. I'll back to try their other items on the menu.

reviews.author: Lup S L
reviews.text: I have tried many hawker and kopitiams wanton mee offerings in the east, central and western region and this is the best  in Singapore so far. It's done Canton style without char siew and the dry version comes with with light spices instead of a thick gravy. The shrimp wanton is professionally wrapped (one look you know its different from other hawker stalls) and the thin noodle is crunchy (not the soggy). I am NOT a big fan of chicken feet but I ordered it anyway because of the thick succulent mushroom nicely braised in a soy-based broth. The hawker centre itself is also my favourite as its usually not very crowded and very well ventilated. I usually finish this with one of the many good standard Chinese dessert stalls offerings or scoop of gelato at the adjacent air-con food court.

reviews.author: Gwen Lim
reviews.text: Thoroughly impressed with their authentic bamboo pole noodles with duck eggs! 🍜 The broth is incredibly flavorful, with a light yet rich taste. The mushroom stew with dried oysters was a perfect complement. A truly delightful and honest meal! 😋💯

reviews.author: Ben Jin Wong
reviews.text:
I ordered Shrimp Wanton Soup Noodles and Chicken Feet with Mushrooms; proceeded to inhale all of them!

Soup was slightly bland, but with the addition of Fried Pork Lard, it was quite tasty! Noodles were springy and the Shrimp Wanton had fresh and massive shrimps! Highlight for me would be the Chicken Feet; they were massive (look at my picture), tender and flavourful!

Well done, and will be back again!

reviews.author: Marie Law
reviews.text:
Sorry no photos of the superb noodles, why because it's good that we devour it the minute it reaches us.
Must try the chicken feets noodle and those mushrooms is soaked in the braised soya sauce that packs a ton of flavour and wanton noodle is so Q tan. It's out of this world , they used those duck egg in their noodles rather than the usual chicken eggs. So the texture is so good. Why should you try it because it's restaurant standard in a hawker that's why. Will definitely come back to try all their other stuff.
